ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general steamed bun class (FOODON_03315021). No specific code exists for chilli crab and pork filled steamed bun. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX covers steamed bread and buns as a category. No specific code for chilli crab pork bao exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific filled steamed bun product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One steamed chilli crab and pork bun weighing approximately 80 g, as typically served at Din Tai Fung and similar Singaporean dim sum establishments.

nutrient_source.title: Steamed Pork Bun (Char Siu Bao / Baozi) — nutrient composition adapted with crab-filling adjustment; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entry for steamed pork bun and HPB Singapore Foodo data for similar dim sum items
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) Foodo / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Foodo or USDA entry exists for a chilli crab and pork steamed bun. Values are adapted from HPB Foodo steamed pork bun (bao) data and USDA FoodData Central NDB 18350 (steamed bread/bun) with adjustment for crab meat filling contribution. The chilli crab sauce component adds modest sodium and sugar. Macros are consistent with a wheat-flour steamed bun (~50% carbohydrate, ~10% protein, ~5% fat per 100 g) with a mixed pork-crab filling. Confidence is medium due to absence of an exact match.

gi_source.title: Steamed wheat bun / baozi GI analogous estimate based on steamed white bread and dim sum bun entries
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a chilli crab and pork steamed bun. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entries for steamed white bread (GI ~64–70) and steamed Chinese buns (baozi, GI ~65, as reported in peer-reviewed literature including Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). The mixed filling of pork and crab is protein-rich and does not substantially alter the GI of the wheat-flour bun wrapper. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 27.6) / 100 ≈ 18.0, using 80 g serving with 34.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~336 mg per bun / ~420 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Each bun contributes approximately 336 mg sodium, representing about 15% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by the American Heart Association; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to one bun per meal and avoid pairing with other high-sodium dim sum items.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 65) and moderate glycemic load (~18 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 65 and a glycemic load of approximately 18 per 80 g bun, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as a moderate-GI carbohydrate food, limiting to one bun per sitting and pairing with non-starchy vegetables or protein to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~1.3 g per bun) and presence of crab meat (omega-3 source)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content per bun is modest at approximately 1.3 g, and the crab meat filling provides a small amount of omega-3 fatty acids; occasional consumption is acceptable within a heart-healthy diet, though sodium intake should be monitored in the context of overall daily dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Steamed Chilli Crab and Pork Bun (辣蟹鲜肉包) is a Singaporean-Chinese fusion dim sum item that pays homage to Singapore's iconic national dish, chilli crab, by encasing a savoury filling of crab meat, minced pork, and spiced chilli crab sauce inside a soft, fluffy steamed bun made from premium wheat flour. The bun is prepared by steaming, which gives the exterior a pillowy, slightly glossy texture while keeping the interior moist and flavourful. The filling delivers a balance of sweet, spicy, and umami notes characteristic of Singapore-style chilli crab sauce, complemented by the richness of minced pork. Popularised by Din Tai Fung at its Singapore outlets, this bun is served as a single piece and is commonly enjoyed as part of a dim sum spread or as a standalone snack. Nutritionally, each 80 g bun provides approximately 174 kcal, 7.6 g protein, 3.8 g fat, and 27.6 g carbohydrate, with a medium glycemic index of 65 and a glycemic load of approximately 18. The sodium content (~336 mg per bun) warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension. The crab meat component contributes a small amount of omega-3 fatty acids and zinc. This item is not halal due to the pork filling and contains gluten and crustacean allergens. It is suitable for those without shellfish or gluten sensitivities and can be incorporated into a balanced diet in moderate portions.

confidence_reason: No exact match found in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for a chilli crab and pork steamed bun. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Foodo steamed pork bun data and USDA SR Legacy steamed bun entries, with adjustment for the crab-meat filling component. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using steamed Chinese bun (baozi) as the closest analogous food. Confidence is medium due to the absence of a direct laboratory-analysed entry for this specific product.
notes: Vitamin A (vitamin_a_mcg_rae) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for steamed buns of this type. Vitamin B6 (vitamin_b6_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Vitamin B12 (vitamin_b12_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Folate (folate_mcg_dfe) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Trans fat is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as steamed buns prepared without hydrogenated fats contain negligible trans fat per USDA SR Legacy data. This product is not halal due to pork filling. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Fish and seafood' (input) to 'Mixed' to reflect the composite wheat-flour bun and mixed pork-crab filling nature of the product. Serving size of 80 g is estimated based on typical Din Tai Fung bun portion weight; actual weight may vary. Glycemic load per serving = (65 × 34.5 × 0.80) / 100 ≈ 18.0.
